topic: Cross-lingual Offensive Language Detection: A Systematic
topic_definition: Cross-lingual offensive language detection is the task of identifying offensive or harmful text in one language by leveraging data, representations, or models transferred from one or more other languages.

summary_topics.description: Cross-lingual Offensive Language Detection: A Systematic

required.criterion: Describes cross-lingual approaches to detect general abusive content (toxic, hate, etc.).
Describes cross-lingual approaches to detect subtypes of abusive content (e.g., sexism, racism).
Describes a multilingual approach with knowledge transfer between languages.
Studies text classification cases in cross-lingual HS (hate speech) scenarios.

exclusion_criteria.criterion: Transfers knowledge across dimensions other than languages (e.g., platforms, domains, datasets).
Targets low-resource languages without employing knowledge transfer approaches.
